hero.title: Multistage Modular Medical Models
hero.subtitle: A Modular Foundation Model Framework for Medical Imaging
hero.desc: The M4 project advances smart healthcare through an AI vision pipeline. Models learn visual features from natural images, build a foundation using multimodal medical images, and are finally fine-tuned into specialized models under physician guidance. This project develops clinical support tools to implement precision medicine while ensuring data privacy.

infographic.title: Technical Highlights
infographic.desc: Through the M4 MEDCLAW platform, we aim to break down the barriers of medical AI development, enabling hospitals to independently create precision medical foundation models.

cards.title: Technical Highlights
cards.desc: Powerful lesion feature extraction and automated fine-tuning without expert intervention.

cards.title: M4 MEDCLAW
cards.desc: Agent-assisted platform for autonomous medical imaging foundation model fine-tuning.

cards.title: Industrial Application
cards.desc: Autonomous generation of proprietary models, clinical decision support, and economic efficiency.

about.title: Project Overview | M4 Modular Medical Foundation Models
about.desc:
This project centers on "Beyond Scaling Law, with Data Efficiency and Trustworthiness at the Core," aiming to build a Modular Medical Foundation Model (M4) framework for medical imaging to address key challenges like data scarcity, high labeling costs, and privacy constraints in real clinical settings.

Traditional AI development relies on massive data and model scaling, which is often impractical in medicine. This project adopts a "Strategy over Scale" mindset, combining Self-Supervised Learning, Multi-stage Transfer Learning, and Task-specific Fine-Tuning to enable AI to progressively learn and establish clinically valuable medical semantic understanding with minimal expert-labeled data.

sections.title: I. Background & Challenges
sections.content:
**• Scarcity**
Medical labeled data is scarce and expensive; stable training and generalization are needed under limited labels.
**• Domain Shift**
Large differences across hospitals/equipment cause inconsistent model performance due to varying data distributions.
**• Adaptation Challenges**
Extended pre-training on distilled large models may damage existing general representations, making adaptation difficult.

sections.title: II. Project Goals & Scope
sections.content:
**• Extended Pre-training Workflow**
Establish workflows suitable for distilled models to improve domain adaptation and reduce degradation risks.
**• Few-shot Training**
Develop few-shot training methods to enhance efficiency under limited labels, using intermediate relevant fine-tuning to improve results.
**• Cross-hospital Validation**
Establish cross-hospital validation mechanisms, introducing doctor-in-the-loop to support clinical review and feedback iteration.

sections.title: III. Core Innovations
sections.content:
**• DAP Medical Domain Adaptation**
Based on large natural image models, use distilled extended pre-training for medical domain enhancement, effectively transferring general image representations to multiple medical modalities.
**• Few-shot & Data Efficiency**
In scenarios with limited labeled data, adopt intermediate fine-tuning and high-efficiency adaptation strategies to achieve stable and generalized performance with fewer labels.
**• Feature Fusion & Global Optimization**
Fuse complementary features from MAE Pixio and DINO, connecting all training modules for global optimization to enhance cross-modal consistency and clinical usability.

sections.title: IV. Technical Process
sections.content:
**1. Self-Supervised Domain Adaptation Pre-training (DAP)**
Pixio (MAE-style) × DINO (v3) | Three modalities: Fundus, Endoscopy, Echo

**2. Intermediate Supervised Fine-tuning**
Intermediate task fine-tuning for MAE Pixio and DINO respectively.

**3. Feature Fusion**
Integrate MAE Pixio × DINO into a single fused model.

**4. End-to-End Optimization**
Global model optimization combined with Doctor-in-the-loop validation feedback.

sections.title: V. Milestones
sections.content: We have set a clear roadmap, from infrastructure construction to final cross-hospital clinical trials, ensuring the project achieves its expected impact step by step.

privacy.title: Your Privacy Matters
privacy.desc: The M4 Project advances smart healthcare through an AI vision pipeline. The model learns visual features from natural images, builds a medical foundation from multimodal imaging, and is fine-tuned into specialized models under physician guidance. The project develops clinical support tools to implement precision medicine while safeguarding data privacy.
privacy.fullPolicy:
Chang Gung University M4 Project Website Privacy Policy
Welcome to the official website of the M4 Project of the Artificial Intelligence Research Center, Chang Gung University (hereinafter referred to as "this website"). This project is jointly executed by relevant research units of the Artificial Intelligence Research Center, Chang Gung University (hereinafter referred to as "the project team"). To protect your privacy and help you understand how this website collects, processes, and utilizes your personal data, please read the following policy description carefully:

I. Scope of Policy Application
This policy applies to the personal data involved when you browse this website, use online services of this website, or participate in online activities related to this project. This policy does not apply to third-party websites linked to this website, nor does it apply to personnel not employed or managed by the project team.

II. Methods and Purposes of Personal Data Collection
Website Browsing Records: When you browse this website, the server automatically generates relevant logs (Log), including your IP address, usage time, browser type, and click records. These data are mainly used for website traffic analysis and system improvement of this project and will not be compared with specific individuals.

Contact and Consultation: If you contact the project through website forms or emails, we will collect your name, email, and communication content, which will only be used to respond to your inquiries or provide project-related information.

Project-Specific Data Processing: * The medical images, clinical data, or AI model training data involved in the M4 project strictly comply with the regulations of the Institutional Review Board (IRB).

All research data and images displayed on this website have undergone de-identification (De-identification) processing to ensure that specific natural persons cannot be identified.

III. Data Protection and Security Measures
This website host is equipped with firewalls, anti-virus systems, and various information security protection measures to ensure the security of your data and the research data of this project.

Only core researchers authorized by this project can access relevant data. All personnel have signed confidentiality agreements and will bear relevant legal and administrative responsibilities in case of violations.

IV. Regulations on Sharing Data with Third Parties
The project team will never provide, exchange, or sell your personal data to other individuals, groups, or private enterprises. Except for the following cases:

With your written consent or active authorization.

Cooperating with investigations conducted by judicial or regulatory agencies according to law.

For academic research purposes, and the data has been processed to the extent that specific parties cannot be identified (complying with the academic exception provisions of Articles 6 and 15 of the Personal Data Protection Act).

V. Technical Application of Cookies
This website uses Cookie technology to provide a more convenient browsing experience. You can refuse access to Cookies through browser settings, but this may cause some functions of the website to not work properly.

VI. Rights of Project Participants
According to the Taiwan "Personal Data Protection Act", you have the rights to inquire, review, request copies, supplement, correct, stop collection, processing, utilization, or request deletion of your personal data collected for this project. If you have relevant needs, please contact us through the official contact channels of this project.

VII. Revision of Privacy Policy
In response to changes in laws or project development needs, this privacy policy will be revised from time to time. The revised terms will be announced immediately on this website without individual notice.

VIII. Contact Us
If you have any questions about the privacy policy or data processing of the M4 Project, please contact:

Project Name: Chang Gung University Artificial Intelligence Research Center M4 Project (M4 Project)

Executing Unit: Chang Gung University Artificial Intelligence Research Center

Official Website: https://m4.cgu.edu.tw/
